fact: Hybrid agent-team v1 architecture created as internal workflow package in projects/_ops/agent-team/. Roles: Marvin (orchestrator), Builder (implementation), Reviewer (quality gate). Built on Antfarm patterns but kept lightweight.

Model routing preferences defined: Marvin uses codex5.4, Builder uses codex, Reviewer uses qwenplus. Note: Environment limitations exist around explicit model routing - fallback to default model may occur.
Communication preference reinforced: Philippe wants clear, honest, structured explanations from the team.
Trial 1 success: Created autonomous-status.py utility via Builder -> Reviewer flow. Reads board.json and executor-last-result.json to report backlog counts and task status.
Trial 2 success: Created launch-path-spec.md and delegation-helper.sh to document preferred launch paths and graceful degradation rules.

Trial 3 (first real backlog run): Created daily-check.html via Builder. Reviewer returned retry due to missing append-only logging. Main session accepted artifact but repaired workflow by adding tasks-log.md entry.
Package polished: Added START-HERE.md, package-map.md, operator-checklist.md as operator-facing entry points. Kept layered summary + detail for weaker model compatibility.
